name: MECONIUM PLUG SYNDROME
definition: local inspissation of meconium leading to low colonic obstruction; probably related to small

demographics.Age: newborn infant (symptomatic within first 24 hours of life)

dominant_finding: small left colon with change in caliber at splenic flexure
distribution: small left colon with change in caliber at splenic flexure
discriminating_features: distended transverse + ascending colon + dilated small bowel (proximal to obstruction)
small left colon with change in caliber at splenic flexure
occasionally bubbly appearance in colon (DDx: submucosal air in necrotizing enterocolitis)
presacral pseudotumor → no gas in rectum
double-contrast effect = barium between meconium plug + colonic wall

general: distended transverse + ascending colon + dilated small bowel (proximal to obstruction)
small left colon with change in caliber at splenic flexure
occasionally bubbly appearance in colon (DDx: submucosal air in necrotizing enterocolitis)
presacral pseudotumor → no gas in rectum
